rules.condition: [Patch rule — melanoma_vs_melanocytic_nevus] Lesion is clearly asymmetric — one half is not a mirror image of the other in shape OR pigment distribution; At least three distinct colors are visible within the lesion (e.g. light brown, dark brown/black, gray-blue, pink-red, white regression area); Border is irregular, notched, or scalloped — not smooth/round/oval; Pigment distribution is heterogeneous (dark patches next to lighter or pink/white areas), not a uniform brown of a banal nevus; DO NOT demand ulceration, bleeding, or clinical history of growth — those are NOT required for a dermoscopic melanoma call; the visual ABCD-style features above are themselves sufficient; An atypical pigment network (broadened, branched, abruptly cut off at the periphery) OR streaks/pseudopods radiating from the edge OR a blue-white veil OR irregular dots/globules unevenly distributed is present — at least one of these melanoma-specific dermoscopic structures; The lesion is NOT a uniform reticular network with regular meshwork and gradual fade-out at the periphery (the classic banal nevus pattern); The lesion does NOT consist of evenly-sized, evenly-distributed brown globules in a symmetric arrangement (the classic globular nevus pattern)
rules.action: Classify as Melanoma. Confidence: high. Rule: When a pigmented lesion is asymmetric AND shows three or more distinct colors (e.g. dark brown plus gray-blue plus pink/red or white) AND has an irregular/notched border, classify as Melanoma. Do NOT require ulceration — these dermoscopic features alone are sufficient evidence of melanoma; nevi are typically symmetric, one-or-two-toned brown, with smooth borders.

rules.condition: [Patch rule — melanoma_vs_melanocytic_nevus] Lesion is clearly asymmetric in shape and pigment distribution; At least three distinct colors are visible (e.g. light brown, dark brown, gray-blue/lavender, pink-red, white); Border is irregular, notched, or scalloped — not smooth/round/oval; Pigment distribution is heterogeneous, not uniform brown of a banal nevus; An atypical pigment network (broadened, branched, abruptly cut off at the periphery) OR streaks/pseudopods OR a blue-white veil OR irregular dots/globules unevenly distributed is present — at least one melanoma-specific dermoscopic structure; The lesion is NOT a uniform reticular network with regular meshwork and gradual fade-out at the periphery (the classic banal nevus pattern); The lesion does NOT consist of evenly-sized, evenly-distributed brown globules in a symmetric arrangement (the classic globular nevus pattern); DO NOT demand ulceration, bleeding, or clinical history of growth — the dermoscopic ABCD-style features above are themselves sufficient
rules.action: Classify as Melanoma. Confidence: high. Rule: Asymmetric multi-lobed lesion with at least three hues (light brown, dark brown, gray-purple/lavender central area, pink). Border is irregular and notched. Pigment is heterogeneous with a broadened atypical-appearing network and a central gray-blue/lavender area suggestive of regression or blue-white veil. Not a uniform reticular or symmetric globular pattern.
